title: Skinner metrological claims verified against QA axioms

witnesses.note: Parker pi derived from integer system, not from continuous pi

witnesses.characteristic_operation: digital root of each letter value
witnesses.qa_connection: 24 = QA applied modulus = pi(9) = min Pisano FP

claims.label: Solar day 5184 = 72^2; 72 = QA Cosmos orbit pair count

witnesses.qa_note: 72 pairs in the 24-cycle Cosmos orbit; 144 = 2 x 72 = Adam

claims.label: Adam=144 and Woman=135 both have digital root 9

difference.note: 144 - 135 = 9; the difference IS the digital root

witnesses.skinner_quote: Adam, and the woman, and the serpent, are one in the center of this garden, as the number 9
witnesses.qa_note: 9 = QA Singularity fixed point (9,9) in mod-9

claims.label: Factor 6 bridges mod-9 theoretical to mod-24 applied

witnesses.qa_note: numerator and denominator digital roots sum to QA modulus
witnesses.skinner_connection: 113 = 144 - 31 (Adam minus El); 355 links via Parker ratio

claims.label: Skinner's system is T2-compliant: pi as observer projection only

witnesses.method: All derivations use integer arithmetic: subtraction (144-31=113), powers (9^4=6561), products (72*72=5184)
witnesses.pi_role: Pi appears ONLY as output comparison (observer projection). 20612/6561 ≈ pi is a MEASUREMENT of the integer system, not an input to it.
witnesses.t2_statement: Float x modulus -> int cast is a QA violation (T2-b). Skinner never does this.
examples: 6561:20612 ratio COMPARED to pi, not DERIVED from pi
witnesses.qa_note: Theorem NT practiced in 1875: discrete arithmetic is primary, continuous pi is observer output

qualified_claims.label: El=31 is NOT a full QA generator (generates only {1,4,7})
qualified_claims.detail: 31 mod 9 = 4; order of 4 in (Z/9Z)* is 3; generates subgroup {1,4,7}, not full group. True generators are 2 and 5 (order 6).

qualified_claims.label: Palindrome dr-preservation is trivially true
qualified_claims.detail: Digit reversal preserves digit sum in any base, hence preserves digital root. This is a base-10 arithmetic identity, not a structural property of the Hebrew system.

qualified_claims.label: Parker pi is mediocre compared to Metius
qualified_claims.detail: 20612/6561 error ≈ 1.23e-6; Metius 355/113 error ≈ 2.67e-7 (about 5x better). The value of Parker's ratio is not its accuracy but that 6561 = 9^4.

falsifiers.description: If El=31 were a full generator of (Z/9Z)*, all residues would be reachable. But 31 mod 9 = 4, order 3, so only {1,4,7} are reachable.

sources: Skinner, Key to the Hebrew-Egyptian Mystery in the Source of Measures (1875)
Parker, The Quadrature of the Circle (in Skinner 1875)
